response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong general software engineering practices and has tackled complex distributed systems, he lacks the specific expertise this role demands. His resume shows minimal Java and Kotlin development (despite listing them as skills), zero Spring ecosystem experience, and no demonstrable work with IDE internals or language tooling. At the mid-senior level, the role requires deep, hands-on expertise in enterprise JVM frameworks and IDE development—areas where his background shows only surface exposure.
strengths: Exemplary software craftsmanship—advocates TDD, clean architecture, and CI/CD; created testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Proven ability to design and scale complex distributed systems (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second; GISCollective platform)
Strong mentorship and communication skills demonstrated through technical workshops, conference talks, and open-source leadership
concerns: Java and Kotlin listed as skills but with negligible project evidence; no substantial backend or server-side work shown
Complete absence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise framework experience—all explicitly required
No background in IDE internals, language tooling, AST manipulation, or debugger/compiler work; only tangential NI LabWindows exposure
